title: Merging Branches
subtitle: Combining your work

sections.instruction: We need a repository with a commit before we can branch and merge. Initialize git first.

onSuccess.message: Base commit saved. Now let's create a feature branch.

sections.instruction: Create a new branch for your feature. You're going to add a footer to the website.
sections.prompt: Type `git checkout -b add-footer` to create and switch to the feature branch.

onSuccess.message: You're now on the 'add-footer' branch. Time to make a change.

sections.instruction: Add a footer to the HTML file. The `>>` operator appends text to a file without erasing what's already there.
sections.prompt: Type `echo "<footer>Copyright 2024</footer>" >> index.html` to add a footer.

onSuccess.message: Footer added to index.html. Now we need to commit this change on the feature branch.

sections.prompt: Type `git add .` to stage the modified file.

sections.instruction: Commit the footer feature on this branch.
sections.prompt: Type `git commit -m "Add footer"` to save this change.

onSuccess.message: Feature committed on the add-footer branch. Now switch back to main to merge it in.

sections.instruction: To merge, you first switch to the branch you want to merge INTO. Switch back to main.
sections.prompt: Type `git checkout main` to switch back to the main branch.

onSuccess.message: You're back on main. Notice the footer is gone from index.html — that change only exists on add-footer. Let's bring it in.

sections.instruction: **Merge** brings the changes from another branch into your current branch. Since you're on main, merging add-footer will bring the footer into main.
sections.prompt: Type `git merge add-footer` to merge the feature branch into main.

onSuccess.message: The footer is now on main! That's the power of branches: experiment safely, then merge when ready.

completionMessage: You just completed the full branch-and-merge workflow: create a branch, make changes, commit, switch back, and merge. This is how professional developers add features without breaking the main codebase.
